concept: Framed as a demo for actors skeptical of AI—showing how motion capture becomes a creative tool, not a replacement. The robot learns YOUR unique movement signature.

shots.frame: Wide: Person (dressed like they're auditioning) flows through yoga, robot learning their specific style—not generic motion, but THEIR motion
shots.audio: Person's voice: 'This isn't replacing me. It's learning from me.'

shots.frame: CU: Dashboard shows 'Learning [Actor Name]'s movement signature... Unique style markers detected: 47'
shots.audio: UI sounds, voice continues: 'Every person moves differently. This captures MY way.'

shots.frame: CU: Robot's hands mirroring the prayer position, ActionTranslator overlay shows: 'Compiling gesture... confidence: 0.94, executing: HAND_POSITION_PRAYER'
shots.audio: Precise servo movements, compilation complete chime

shots.frame: MCU: Person does a unique flourish in the pose—robot mirrors it perfectly, capturing their artistic choice
shots.audio: Person laughs: 'It even got my weird hand thing! That's actually impressive.'

shots.frame: ECU: Person's face, genuinely impressed, nodding thoughtfully
shots.audio: 'So this is local motion capture... I own this data. It's MY performance.'

shots.frame: Wide: Person and robot, text overlay: 'Your movement. Your data. Your control. StepbyStep:ROBOTICS'
shots.audio: 'I could see using this for rehearsal, for choreography... for keeping my work mine.'

invisibleWide: This addresses the core fear: AI replacing creative workers. Instead, it shows AI as a tool that amplifies and preserves individual artistry. Local processing means actors own their motion data. The system learns their unique style, not a generic template.

stormCloud.detail: The dashboard shows 'Data stored locally. No cloud upload. You own this.' in small text.

platform.hook: Actor's skeptical voice immediately frames the tension
platform.loop: Yes — the transformation from skepticism to acceptance is satisfying
platform.soundOff: Poor — the voiceover is essential to the pitch
